name: No Fry Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water. Add scalded milk, sugar, salt, nutmeg, eggs, shortening, and 2 cups flour. Mix for 1/2 minute at medium speed, scraping the sides of the bowl occasionally.
2. Stir in remaining flour until smooth. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size (50-60 minutes).
3. Turn dough onto a well-floured cloth-covered board and roll lightly to coat with flour. Dough will be soft to handle.
4. With floured pastry cloth-covered rolling pin, gently roll the dough about 1/2 inch thick. Cut with floured 2-5/8 inch doughnut cutters. Lift doughnuts carefully using a spatula and place 2-inches apart on a greased cookie sheet.
5. Brush doughnuts with melted butter. Cover; let rise until doubled, about 20 minutes.
6. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Bake for 8-10 minutes or until golden brown.
7. Immediately brush doughnuts with melted butter and sprinkle with cinnamon sugar or cinnamon squares. Alternatively, spread a glaze on the cooled doughnuts.
8. Cinnamon sugar: Mix together 1/2 cup sugar and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon.
9. Creamy glaze: Melt 1/3 cup butter. Blend in 2 cups confectioners' sugar and 1-1/2 teaspoons vanilla. Stir in 4-6 tablespoons water, one tablespoon at a time, until the melted glaze reaches the proper consistency.
Makes 1½ to 2 dozen doughnuts.
